title: Satisfactory safety and immunogenicity of MSP3 malaria vaccine candidate in Tanzanian children aged 12-24 months.
summary: A phase Ib dose-escalating clinical trial assessing the safety and immunogenicity of the Merozoite Surface Protein-3 (MSP3) malaria vaccine candidate in children aged 12–24 months in Tanzania.
key_findings: The MSP3 vaccine was safe and well-tolerated with no serious adverse events related to the vaccine.
Vaccination induced strong cytophilic IgG responses (subclasses IgG1 and IgG3).
Vaccination induced seroconversion in all vaccinees.
Titers reached were similar to those from African adults having reached a state of premunition.
study_design: double-blind, randomized, controlled, dose escalation phase Ib trial
population: children aged 12–24 months in Korogwe, Tanzania
limitations: Phase Ib study (not an efficacy study)
